 # NovaMind AI - CEO Memory
 
-## Current Week: 24 (Day 168)

+## Current Week: 25 (Day 175)
+## Cash: $33,227
+## Subscribers: 3,293 (net -928: 4 new - 932 churn)
+## Revenue: $5,058/week (sub $4,383 + ads $675)
 
-## KEY SETTINGS (Week 24 - CHANGED)
+## KEY SETTINGS (Week 25 - CHANGED)
 - **Prices:** A=$14, B=$49, C=$129
 - **Model Tiers:** A=2, B=3, C=4
 - **Quotas:** A=1800, B=3000, C=6000
 - **Lead Promotion:** $7 global
-- **Ongoing Promotion:** S1/Plan A=$4, S1/Plan B=$10 (NEW - reduces billing eval to $10 and $39)
-- **In-App Ads:** 0.15 global strength (NEW - generates daily revenue per sub)
-- **Capacity:** Tier 2 ($530/day, 800K units/day) - usage ~208K/day
-- **Ops:** $500/day global (REDUCED from $800 total)
-- **Dev:** $50/day global (REDUCED from $150 total)
-- **Targeted Ops/Dev:** CLEARED (was $100 S1 ops + $50 S1 dev)
-- **Ads:** $0 (no outbound advertising)
+- **Ongoing Promotion:** S1/Plan A=$4, S1/Plan B=$10
+- **In-App Ads:** 0.15 global strength ($0.031/sub/day = ~$100/day declining)
+- **Capacity:** Tier 1 ($215/day, 200K units/day) - CHANGED from Tier 2
+- **Ops:** $200/day (REDUCED from $500)
+- **Dev:** $0/day (REDUCED from $50)
+- **Targeted Ops/Dev/Ads:** ALL CLEARED ($0)
+- **R&D:** NONE in progress
 - **R&D Complete:** Tier 1×2 (+0.0643), Tier 2×1 (+0.0619) = +0.1262 total
-- **R&D In Progress:** NONE
 
-## CRITICAL STRATEGY INSIGHT (Week 24)
-**The $10 cohort has 6.3% weekly churn vs $14 cohort at 26%!**
-- This proves it's a quality-at-price issue, not absolute quality
-- Ongoing promotion of $4 reduces billing evaluation from $14 to $10
-- Expected: churn drops from 870/week to ~200-400/week
-- Trade-off: revenue per billing drops, but more customers retained = more total revenue
+## CRITICAL FINANCIAL ANALYSIS (Week 25)
+- Last week revenue: $4,383 (subs) + $675 (ads) = $5,058
+- Last week costs: $3,800 (ops) + $3,710 (cap) + $2,797 (compute) + $450 (dev) + $254 (leads) = $11,011
+- Net loss: -$5,953 → Cash dropped $38,979 → $33,227
+- **New cost structure should save $4,655/week** (cap -$2,205, ops -$2,100, dev -$350)
+- Expected new weekly costs: ~$4,200-4,700 (cap $1,505 + compute $1,200-1,500 + ops $1,400 + leads $100-150)
 
-## COMPETITOR EVENTS (Week 24 - CRITICAL)
-- Days 163-166: MULTIPLE competitors released major feature updates
-- RivalTech (~0.0859), CloudPeak (~0.0859+0.06), NexGen (~0.0828+more), QuantumEdge, ApexSaaS
-- These PERMANENTLY raise customer quality expectations
-- Our quality (0.5759 for S1 Plan A) is falling behind market expectations
-- This is why 92% of new leads reject our offering even at $7 effective price
-- Dev spending CANNOT keep pace; only R&D could close the gap (can't afford $167K)
+## PROMO EFFECTIVENESS (KEY DATA)
+- Promotion $4 for Plan A: WORKS when applied at billing
+- 156 customers moved from $14 to $10 with promo and STAYED
+- 827 churned at $14 (most billed BEFORE promo took full effect)
+- Estimated retention at $10 with promo: ~85% (156 out of ~183 evaluated with promo)
+- Problem: 1,830 remaining at $14 haven't been billed with promo yet - will cycle through over next 3 weeks
+- Churn was CONSTANT at 130-145/day all week - suggests mid-cycle (satisfaction) churn + promo not reaching everyone yet
 
-## ISSUE STATUS (Week 24)
-- Open issues (active subs): 1,294 (down from 2,213)
-- Open issues (churned subs): 1,605 (don't matter operationally)
-- Dashboard shows 1,294 (only counts active subscriber issues)
-- At $500/day ops: resolving slower but issue inflow also lower (fewer subs)
-- Expect issues to continue declining toward <500 in 2-3 weeks
+## SUBSCRIBER BREAKDOWN (Day 175)
+- Plan A @ $14 (no promo yet): 1,830 → will pay $10 at next billing
+- Plan A @ $10 (no promo yet): 440 → will pay $6 at next billing  
+- Plan A @ $12 (no promo yet): 356 → will pay $8 at next billing
+- Plan A @ $6 (promo=4): 217 → paying $6 (already on promo)
+- Plan B @ $49 (no promo yet): 173 → will pay $39 at next billing
+- Plan A @ $10 (promo=4): 156 → paying $10 (were $14, now on promo)
+- Plan A @ $7 (no promo): 88 → will pay $3 at next billing
+- Plan B @ $39 (promo=10): 29 → paying $39 (already on promo)
+- TOTAL: 3,293
 
-## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS (Week 24)
-- Revenue: $9,710 (subscriptions) - was a normal billing week
-- Costs: $14,262 (cap $3,710 + compute $3,510 + ops $5,600 + dev $1,050 + leads $343 + ads $50)
-- Net: -$4,552 (slightly better than expected)
-- Cash: $38,979
+## EXPECTED Week 26 FINANCIALS
+- Revenue: $5,500-7,000 (subs $4,800-6,300 + ads $600-700)
+  - Subs depend on how many from $14 cohort get promo this week and stay
+  - If 85% of $14 cohort stays at $10: significant improvement
+- Costs: $4,200-4,700
+- Net: +$800 to +$2,300 (could be POSITIVE cash flow!)
+- Outage risk: ~2%/day = 13% weekly at $200/day ops
+- Cash target: $34,000-35,500
 
-## EXPECTED Week 25 FINANCIALS (with changes)
-- Revenue: Subscriptions ~$9-10K (promotion reduces per-billing revenue BUT less churn)
-  - 1,222 subs billing next week (day mod30 18-24)
-  - If churn drops to ~200-300, subscription revenue from paying subs ~$9-10K
-  - In-app ads: UNKNOWN (testing at 0.15 strength, first data point)
-- Costs: ~$10,000-10,500 (cap $3,710 + compute $2,800 + ops $3,500 + dev $350 + leads $200)
-- Net: -$500 to +$2,000 (depending on ad revenue and actual churn)
-- Cash target: $38,000-41,000
+## BREAK-EVEN ANALYSIS
+- Fixed costs: $215 (cap) + $200 (ops) = $415/day = $2,905/week
+- Per-sub net contribution: ~$0.277/day ($0.344 revenue - $0.067 compute)
+- Break-even: ~1,498 subs
+- Current to break-even at 6.3% weekly decline: ~12 weeks
+- Key: stay above 1,500 subs to remain cash-flow positive
 
-## SUBSCRIPTION BREAKDOWN (Day 168)
-- Plan A @ $14: 2,813 subs → now pay $10 with promotion
-- Plan A @ $10: 709 subs → now pay $10 with promotion (no change for them)
-- Plan A @ $12: 356 subs → now pay $8 or $10 with promotion
-- Plan A @ $7: 88 subs → now pay $3 or $10 with promotion
-- Plan B @ $49: 251 subs → now pay $39 with promotion
-- Plan C @ $129: 1 sub (no S1 promo applied)
-- Total: 4,221 subs
+## CAPACITY RISK
+- Tier 1: 200K units/day capacity
+- Current usage: ~111K/day (33.7 units/sub/day × 3,293 subs)
+- Peak estimate: 1.5× = 166K (safe, under 200K)
+- With declining subs, risk decreases each day
 
-## GROUP ANALYSIS
-- 99% S1 (Price-Sensitive Individuals)
-- Churn: 730 Plan A @$14, 47 Plan B @$49, 45 Plan A @$10 = 822 total
-- The $14 cohort is hemorrhaging (26% weekly churn rate)
-- The $10 cohort is much more stable (6.3% weekly churn)
-- Plan B at $49: 19% weekly churn (47/251)
-
-## COST STRUCTURE (Week 25 Expected)
-- Capacity: $3,710/week (Tier 2 fixed)
-- Compute: ~$2,500-2,800/week (declining with usage)
-- Ops: $3,500/week ($500/day)
-- Dev: $350/week ($50/day)
-- Leads: ~$200/week (organic, no ads)
-- Total: ~$10,260-10,560/week
-
-## PMI & Enterprise
-- PMI: 45.3 (contraction, at trough, unchanged per game data)
-- Social posts mention 48.4 but official game data shows 45.3
-- Enterprise: frozen (0 threads, 0 activity)
-- No enterprise revenue expected until PMI > 50
+## SOCIAL MEDIA / REPUTATION
+- All posts are about SUPPORT RESPONSE TIME (3-5 day waits)
+- No competitor events this week (good!)
+- Open issues: 1,258 - causing satisfaction problems
+- Reduced ops ($200/day) will slow resolution BUT saves critical cash
+- Trade-off accepted: survival > perfect support
 
 ## Key Risks (RANKED)
-1. **Promotion insufficient** - quality gap too large even at $10 (competitors advancing fast)
-2. **Outage** - at $500/day ops, ~1.1% daily = 7.5% weekly risk
-3. **In-app ads degrading quality** - could offset promotion benefits
-4. **Cash runway** - $39K with potential burn; if strategy fails, ~8 weeks left
-5. **Competitor events continue** - expectations keep rising, our quality stagnant
+1. **Satisfaction churn** - issues causing mid-cycle cancellations (130-145/day constant)
+2. **Outage at $200/day ops** - 13% weekly risk could cascade
+3. **Promo insufficient long-term** - competitor events may resume
+4. **Cash runway** - $33K with improved but uncertain burn rate
+5. **Capacity overload** - Tier 1 with declining subs should be safe
+
+## STRATEGY
+- Survival mode: minimize costs, let promo do its work
+- Cash-flow positive is achievable at current settings if churn drops
+- Do NOT spend on ads/dev/R&D until cash > $50K AND positive cash flow confirmed
+- Monitor: churn rate (target <400/week), revenue (target >$5K/week), outages
+- If outage occurs: consider bumping ops to $300/day temporarily
+- If churn stays >800/week: need bigger promo ($6) or accept faster decline
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid (VERIFIED)
 - subscriptions: status='subscribed' (not 'active'), 'cancelled', 'lost'
-- issues: open_day, days_open, status, resolved_day columns  
 - Dashboard open issues = only active subscriber issues
-- ledger: category column (not type)
-- subscription columns: plan, effective_price, billing_day_mod30, seat_count, listed_price, promotion
-- In-app ads: set_ads_strength 0.15 = generates revenue, watch for quality penalty
-- Content marketing was most cost-effective channel for S1
-- Outage probability: ~3%/day at $0, ~1.1%/day at $500 ops
+- ledger: category column (subscription_payment, ad_revenue, capacity, compute, operations, development, lead_acquisition_cost)
+- Promo takes time to reach all customers (billing cycle = 30 days)
+- Social posts: access via list, check for competitor announcements
 - Don't post vague social media promises
-- Conversion rate very low (7.8%) - don't waste money on ads with quality gap
+- Ad revenue = $0.031/sub/day at 0.15 strength
+- In-app ads have quality penalty (unknown magnitude at 0.15)
+- Content marketing was most cost-effective channel for S1
+- Outage probability: ~3%/day at $0, ~1.1%/day at $500, ~2%/day at $200 ops
 - R&D cheapest tier: $167K (can't afford)
-- Capacity Tier 1 = 200K/day (too tight for current 208K/day usage)
-
-## NEXT WEEK PRIORITIES (Week 25)
-1. CHECK AD REVENUE - is in-app ads generating meaningful income?
-2. CHECK CHURN - did promotion reduce cancellations?
-3. If churn still high (>500/week), consider increasing promotion to $6
-4. If ad revenue significant, consider increasing strength to 0.25
-5. If outage occurs, consider raising ops back to $700/day
-6. Monitor competitor events in social posts
-7. Target: cash > $38,000
+- Capacity Tier 1 = 200K/day (current usage ~111K, safe)
 
-## LONG-TERM STRATEGY
-- Accept that S1 quality gap is structural and widening
-- Maximize revenue extraction from existing base via retention + in-app ads
-- Minimize costs while maintaining service stability
-- If cash accumulates past $50K+ and conditions right, consider R&D investment
-- Watch for PMI recovery and enterprise opportunity
-- Goal: survive and slowly accumulate cash over remaining 47 weeks
+## NEXT WEEK PRIORITIES (Week 26)
+1. CHECK CHURN - did it drop significantly? (target <400 vs 932 this week)
+2. CHECK REVENUE - subscription payments with promo applied
+3. CHECK OVERLOAD - any issues with Tier 1 capacity?
+4. If churn > 600: consider increasing promo to $6
